creator: mason@blisses.org
text:
No, unfortunately, I haven't managed it yet. I've got a HP LaserJet Professional P1109w here, connected by USB. Under various Linux systems (Slackware, CentOS, Ubuntu) the printer's detected and can be used. Under FreeBSD, 'hp-setup -i' is unable to download the PPD.

Also unfortunate, since I have the PPD from other systems where I've been able to configure the printer, I tried supplying that directly after hp-setup failed to download it on its own, but that didn't seem to work either - the printer wasn't registered with CUPS. Adding it manually and specifying the PPD was also unsuccessful - printing gave me a filter error and didn't emit output. Some of this is likely outside the scope of the hplip package, but part of the larger problem, so I'm just focussing on the hplip bits for now. It seems like hplip should be able to successfully download PPD files, and it seems to have some dependencies that aren't specified formally - wget being an example. Maybe the port needs a patch to use fetch instead, or maybe just a dependency on wget, but it seemed worth mentioning.
